famous quote:
"For you will certainly carry out God's purpose, however you act, but it makes a difference to you whether you serve like Judas or like John."
-- C.S. Lewis (The Problem of Pain)
Interview Text:
Bob:

Bob led a silent retreat I went on to try and hear God's voice. 

In his talk to us on the last day of the retreat, Bob told us this story. 

He knew a lady who he labeled as a whirling dervish. She was a woman who could do anything and couldn't stop working on something until it was fixed. She had 6 kids with her husband and was involved in every one of their activities along the way. Her name was Mary Joe.

Then Bob revealed that this had been his wife. At one point, in the middle of her busy, task-accomplishing life, she stepped out of her car and for some reason her legs couldn't hold her up and she collapsed. Within the next few weeks other symptoms started appearing. She couldn't move her muscles like she used to be able to use them. They went in and did some tests and found out her muscles were only recieving %50 of the messages from her brain. But the doctor didn't know what it could be. They went to another doctor to get a second opinion. He said he didn't know either, but that he would do some more tests.

So Mary Joe, true to her nature, took it on herself to solve the problem. When she went back in to see the results of her tests she said, "Doctor, is it possibly Lou Gehrig's disease?"

The doctor said, "Who told you that?" 

She had figured it out, and then went on, like was in her nature to do everything she could to fix it.

But no matter what she did she just got worse. This woman who had always had everything she needed within herself to get things done, was having her abilities slowly taken away. 

She went through the stages of grief including denial and anger. But as she arrived at the place where her husband had to do everything for her, even the most intimate of things it seems only proper for one do do for themself, this woman came to a place of peace. 

She communicated via a keyboard by looking at letters with her eyes. And one day, as Bob sat beside her in sorrow and frustration, looking at everything the disease had taken from her, he asked her, "Hunny, do you wish you had just died quickly instead of having to go through all of this?"

She typed, "No. This disease has become my friend. I wouldn't have this relationship with Jesus without this disease. And I wouldn't share this intimate love with you."

One day near the end she started to type something to him. She asked him if he would take her to a monastery on the East side of Atlanta where they could go and pray. Bob interrupted her, resisting, feeling very frustrated, distant and angry with God. But each time she would start completely over, as was true to her determined character, and request that he go with her.

So Bob says he probably dragged his feet and drove as slow as he could on his way to the monastery. When they got there he resentfully unloaded her and her wheelchair and they went in to one of the services together.

And it was in the middle of one of these services that Bob saw a light appear high up in the corner of the sanctuary. And then he heard a clear voice as if someone was speaking to him, which said, "Don't you think I can use even this? Won't you let yourself be part of my plan?"

Mary Joe passed away. But she had touched lives by the way she had lived through the pain. There was a business man who had been convicted of a white collar crime and was telling his counselor it would be better to end his life than to let himelf be convicted of this and bare all of the shame. 

When the counselor heard this he pointed a finger at him and sternly said, "You have no right to say that. I need to tell you about a woman." That woman was Mary Joe.

And there were women in Mary Joe's Bible study, even when she was completely immobile and barely able to communicate, who came up to tell her she had saved their marriages because they had seen how she responded to her difficulties.

* * *

Bob's story is like some of the other stories I've recorded. He heard a very clear, almost audible voice one strong time. And it didn't come when Bob thought he needed to hear it, like I had thought I needed on the retreat. Bob had felt deserted by and angry at God, but it turns out God had been there the whole time, working through all of it, just waiting for him to turn his face back and come along side to let him use him.

Now Bob volunteers at several places like Ignatius House, telling his story and helping people like myself, believe God is always there guiding me, whether I hear him or not.
Music Video Instructions: Imagine if God's love for us, and ours for him was like this.
